name: Gingerbread from Lucerne (Luzern)
recipeInstructions:
(Units: 100 g = 1/2 oz; 1 dl = 1/2 fl oz = 1/5 cup; 180°F = 50°C;
200°F = 90°C; 230°F = 110°C; 250°F = 120°C; 2.5 cm = 1 inch)
Gingerbread from Lucerne, the story:
There has always been a place for gingerbread on market stalls and at
fairs. It was customary for a young man to offer his sweetheart a
gingerbread cake, which she would accept with delight. Since the Middle
Ages, Lucerne has seen the creation of a large number of such delicacies
influenced by its governors and merchants.
The recipe:
1. Add the lemon juice to the cream and let it sit for a few minutes.
2. Mix together the cream, pear puree, sugar, mixed spices (star aniseed,
cloves, cinnamon, ginger), and bicarbonate of soda in a bowl.
3. Gradually add the whole wheat flour, stirring until well combined.
4. Fold in the roughly crushed walnuts.
5. Pour the batter into a flan ring with a 24 cm diameter and a height
of 6 cm.
6. Bake in an oven preheated to 190°C (375°F) for 50 minutes.
7. Allow to cool before serving cold.
